test: IOWarp CTE Integration Test (full, both environments)

test_environments.result: shared memory bug — all RPC calls timeout 60s
same shared memory bug — all RPC calls timeout 60s

what_works: Chimaera runtime starts and creates CTE pools (cte_main, ram::chi_default_bdev)
Client connects to runtime via ZMQ on port 9416
All Python API signatures are accessible and well-documented
Read-only metadata operations (GetBlobSize, GetContainedBlobs) return responses
Query APIs are exposed: BlobQuery(tag_regex, blob_regex), TagQuery(tag_regex)
what_doesnt_work: PutBlob — Recv times out after 60s (no data actually stored)
Warning from runtime: 'Could not access shared header during ClientInit'
Confirmed in two environments: host machine + iowarp/iowarp Docker with 4GB shm
root_cause: iowarp-core 1.0.3 shared memory IPC bug between runtime and client processes. Reproduced in both native Linux and official Docker image.

iowarp_apis_clio_would_use_for_search: Client.BlobQuery(tag_regex, blob_regex) — find blobs matching a pattern
Client.TagQuery(tag_regex) — find tags matching a regex
Tag.GetContainedBlobs() — list blobs under a tag (metadata only)
Tag.GetBlobSize(name) — blob metadata, no data movement
what_this_means_for_clio.clios_value_at_10B_blob_scale: CLIO extracts scientific metadata (units, measurements, formulas) from blobs during ingestion and stores it in a fast index. At query time, CLIO uses its index to identify the small subset of blob IDs that match the query, then fetches ONLY those blobs from CTE via BlobQuery/GetBlob. No brute-force scanning.
what_this_means_for_clio.proof_of_principle: sampling_test.json shows CLIO inspects 0.006% of a 50,000-blob corpus (3 out of 50,000) to find all matches. The same index-backed filtering applies at 10B scale: CLIO never reads blobs it doesn't need.

conclusion: The integration path is validated at the API level — iowarp-core exposes the exact blob-search primitives CLIO needs (BlobQuery, TagQuery, GetContainedBlobs, GetBlobSize). Full data-plane validation requires either (a) a fixed iowarp-core release without the shared memory bug, or (b) a deployment on an HPC system where the shared memory IPC works. The search-side logic (what CLIO would call) is demonstrated on 50,000 blobs in sampling_test.py with 99.99% inspection reduction.
